seoTitle: Multiple Correct Answers in Microsoft Forms Quizzes
metaDescription: Learn how to let quiz-takers pick more than one correct choice in Microsoft Forms, assign points, and grade the combination automatically.
intro: Single-choice questions fall short when a quiz needs more than one right answer, whether you're certifying technical skills or grading a compliance test. This guide shows how to switch a Choice question into a multi-answer format in Microsoft Forms so the built-in grading engine checks every valid combination for you.

steps.heading: Start from a quiz, not a plain form
steps.body: Open your quiz project and add a new question using the Choice question type, since answer keys and points only apply inside a Forms quiz.

steps.heading: Turn on multiple answers
steps.body: Find the Multiple Answers toggle near the bottom of the question block and switch it on. This swaps the single-select radio buttons for checkboxes.

steps.heading: Mark every correct option
steps.body: Hover over each valid response and click its correct-answer checkmark. You can flag as many options as apply to that question.

steps.heading: Set the point value and require an answer
steps.body: Enter how many points the question is worth in the points field, and toggle Required so the question can't be skipped.

steps.heading: Preview and test
steps.body: Use the Preview button to submit a mock response on both desktop and mobile, confirming the checkboxes register and the score calculates correctly.

faq.question: Do I need a quiz instead of a regular form?
faq.answer: Yes — automated correct-answer grading and points only exist in the Quiz format, not a standard Form.

faq.question: Can respondents get partial credit for picking some but not all correct options?
faq.answer: Forms grades the question as a whole based on your point setting, so review your grading expectations before publishing.

faq.question: Will Multiple Answers work on mobile devices?
faq.answer: Yes, the checkbox layout is responsive and displays correctly on both desktop and mobile preview.

faq.question: Can I go back and edit correct answers after responses come in?
faq.answer: You can edit the answer key at any time, but scores already given won't automatically recalculate unless you review responses again.
